text.tspan: Samsung
SK hynix
Micron
SanDisk
Kioxia
YMTC
Latest public QLC NAND /
status
9th-gen V-NAND QLC, in mass production. Samsung’s ISSCC
disclosure describes a 280-layer, 1Tb, 4b/cell 3D NAND die.
321-layer 2Tb QLC NAND, announced as the first QLC product
above 300 layers; commercial cSSD supply began in 2026.
G9 QLC NAND, shipping in SSDs; Micron calls it the industry’s first
ninth-generation QLC NAND shipping to OEMs in an SSD.
Uses BiCS8 QLC CBA NAND and wraps it in its UltraQLC SSD
platform. SanDisk and Kioxia share the BiCS technology lineage, so
this is not an independent raw NAND node from Kioxia in the same
way Samsung/Micron/YMTC are.
BiCS8 QLC with CBA, in mass production as 2Tb QLC devices and
shipping in LC9 Series SSDs
Xtacking 4.0 X4-6080 2Tb QLC, publicly described as mass-produced
in 2025 and used in PC SSDs.
Die / process specs
disclosed
Public ISSCC spec: 1Tb, 280 layers, 28.5Gb/mm² areal density,
3.2GB/s high-speed I/O. Samsung also highlights double-stack
channel-hole etching and improved retention. (TechInsights)
2Tb die, 321 layers, six planes versus four previously, and 32-die
package support for high-capacity enterprise SSDs. (SK hynix
Newsroom)
2Tb QLC die, six-plane architecture, and up to 3.6GB/s I/O transfer
rate. (Micron)
UltraQLC uses BiCS8 2Tb QLC die technology with platform-level
features including Direct Write QLC, Dynamic Frequency Scaling,
controller changes, and data-retention management. (Sandisk)
2Tb QLC die, CBA — CMOS directly bonded to array, 3.6Gbps
interface speed, and 16-die stacking for a 4TB package. (KIOXIA)
2Tb QLC, Xtacking 4.0 hybrid-bonded architecture, with memory
array and periphery made on separate wafers. YMTC’s public
materials emphasize high bit density and high I/O speed, but do not
disclose a clean public layer-count/areal-density table for X4-6080.
(YMTC)
Speed, power, density
claims
Samsung claims about 86% higher bit density versus its previous
QLC generation, 2x write performance, 60% higher I/O speed, and
read/write power reductions of about 30% / 50%. (Samsung
Semiconductor Global)
SK hynix claims 2x data-transfer speed, up to 56% higher write
performance, 18% higher read performance, and more than 23%
better write power efficiency versus the previous generation. (SK
hynix Newsroom)
Micron emphasizes the combination of 2Tb die density, six-plane
parallelism, and very high NAND I/O. Its G9 QLC is already used in
both client and data-center SSD families. (Micron)
SanDisk claims Dynamic Frequency Scaling can deliver about 10%
higher performance at the same power, and its data-retention
profile can reduce DR recycles by up to 33%. (Sandisk)
Kioxia claims 2.3x bit density and 70% higher write power efficiency
versus its fifth-generation QLC. BiCS9 introduces a new 4.8Gb/s
class Toggle DDR6 interface logic layer bonded via CBA to existing
cell arrays, and BiCS10 pairs that interface generation with a 332-
layer array. (KIOXIA)
YMTC’s PC42Q SSD discloses a 3,600MT/s NAND interface, up to
7,000MB/s read, 6,500MB/s write, up to 900K random-read/write
IOPS, active power below roughly 4–4.5W, and endurance of 150 /
300 / 600TBW for 512GB / 1TB / 2TB. (YMTC)
Representative products /
ramp
Samsung says V9 QLC will cover branded consumer SSDs, UFS, PC
SSDs, and server SSDs, but the cleanest public die-level spec is still
the 1Tb V9 QLC disclosure rather than a named 2Tb QLC die.
(Samsung Semiconductor Global)
First applied to PQC21 client SSDs in 1TB and 2TB capacities, with
Dell supply announced in April 2026. SK hynix plans expansion into
enterprise SSDs and UFS. (SK hynix Newsroom)
Micron 3610 client SSD: PCIe Gen5, up to 4TB, up to 11,000MB/s
read, 9,300MB/s write, and up to 1.5M/1.6M random read/write
IOPS. Micron 6600 ION data-center SSD: up to 245.76TB usable /
256TB raw, PCIe Gen5, E3.L/E3.S options (E3.S limited to 30.72TB to
122.88TB). (Micron)
PC SN5100S: BiCS8 QLC CBA client SSD, PCIe Gen4, up to 2TB, up to
7,300MB/s sequential read. SanDisk also demonstrated 128TB
SN670 and 256TB UltraQLC enterprise SSDs, with U.2 availability
targeted for H1 2026 in its announcement. (Sandisk)
LC9 122.88TB NVMe SSD uses BiCS8 2Tb QLC, PCIe 5.0, dual-port,
0.3 DWPD. Kioxia also announced LC9 245.76TB SSDs using 32-die
stacks of 2Tb BiCS QLC and an 8TB BGA package. (KIOXIA)
PC42Q: PCIe Gen4 x4 NVMe 2.0 client SSD, 512GB / 1TB / 2TB, built
on X4-6080 QLC. YMTC’s prior 232-layer QLC die was independently
seen by TechInsights in a commercial SSD and measured at
19.8Gb/mm², giving useful context for its QLC density trajectory.
(YMTC)
